prevalence.source: Indian Academy of Neurology / IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging and better recognition in urban centers

burden.mortalityRate: Increasing with age; complications contribute to mortality

vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited neurology access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis per MDS criteria; levodopa responsiveness supportive; imaging used mainly to exclude secondary causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; medication access is better for generics, but rehab and advanced therapy access is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist evaluation concentrated in urban tertiary centers; many cases managed in general medicine settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Levodopa and dopamine agonists (generics available); physiotherapy and speech therapy; DBS available in select tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Neurologist scarcity, out-of-pocket costs for rehab, and limited access to DBS/advanced therapy outside metros
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic levodopa and expanding tertiary movement disorder services in major cities
